title: > IMHO, this risks turning ARC-AGI-3 into more of a long-context This is a feature, not a bug ARC-AGI-3 will force memory, compression, and continual learning There is too much information to fit in a context window. The agent will be required to learn what to remember and what to forget

content.summary: > IMHO, this risks turning ARC-AGI-3 into more of a long-context This is a feature, not a bug ARC-AGI-3 will force memory, compression, and continual learning There is too much information to fit in a context window. The agent will be required to learn what to remember and what to forget Maxence Frenette (@maxencefrenette) The addition of animations really changes my assumptions about the type of environments that are possible. Will the agents be able to see these? How will this be communicated to them? IMHO, this risks turning ARC-AGI-3 into more of a long-context/video benchmark. — https://nitter.net/maxencefrenette/status/2035374034217808317#m
content.full_text:
Post title: > IMHO, this risks turning ARC-AGI-3 into more of a long-context This is a feature, not a bug ARC-AGI-3 will force memory, compression, and continual learning There is too much information to fit in a context window. The agent will be required to learn what to remember and what to forget

Will the agents be able to see these? How will this be communicated to them?
